negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The pool extracts max_poll_block and uses settlement_block + max_poll_block as the Phase 2 deadline, but no 100-block buffer constant or addition is present.
related_but_insufficient_code: AwaitingDelivery entries skip wall-clock expiry and DeliveryReady entries use delivery_ready_timestamp.
phase2_deadline_offset extracts the precompile-specific max_poll_block field.
mark_awaiting_delivery extends contract_expiry_block to settlement_block + max_poll_block only.

notes: Confirmed gap: the wall-clock state behavior is present, but the block TTL buffer required by the invariant is absent.
rationale: The async pool extracts max_poll_block from 0x0805 input, adds PHASE2_TTL_BUFFER_BLOCKS, skips time expiry for AwaitingDelivery, and starts time expiry from delivery_ready_timestamp for DeliveryReady. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Long-running async jobs in AwaitingDelivery must not expire by wall-clock txpool lifetime while the executor is polling, DeliveryReady jobs must use time-based expiry from delivery result submission, and Phase 2 block TTL must use max_poll_block plus a 100-block buffer.
suggested_falsification_tests: Advance time beyond max_tx_lifetime for an AwaitingDelivery job and assert it is not time-expired; submit a delivery result and assert the DeliveryReady entry expires only after max_tx_lifetime from submission; advance block number beyond max_poll_block + 100 and assert block TTL expires it.
summary: Long-running TTL handling is missing the 100-block Phase 2 buffer.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set max_poll_block to N, mark Phase 1 settled at block B, and assert current contract_expiry_block is B+N rather than the invariant-required B+N+100.
title: Long-running TTL is Phase-aware
